self.description: Description of the project under analysis

skip.description: Set of rules to skip files from fingerprinting and scanning

scanning.description:
List of glob patterns (gitignore format) to exclude files from scanning. Patterns are matched relative to the scan root directory.

- A trailing `/` matches a directory
- `*` matches anything except `/`
- `**` matches zero or more directories
- `[0-9]` matches character ranges
- `?` matches a single character except `/`
- Leading `!` negates a pattern

fingerprinting.description:
List of glob patterns (gitignore format) to exclude files from fingerprinting. Patterns are matched relative to the scan root directory.

- A trailing `/` matches a directory
- `*` matches anything except `/`
- `**` matches zero or more directories
- `[0-9]` matches character ranges
- `?` matches a single character except `/`
- Leading `!` negates a pattern

sizes.description: Set of rules to skip files based on their size.

scanning.description: Rules for skipping files based on their size during scanning. Each rule combines glob patterns with min/max byte thresholds.

patterns.description: List of glob patterns to apply the min/max size rule

min.description: Minimum size of the file in bytes

max.description: Maximum size of the file in bytes

fingerprinting.description: Rules for skipping files based on their size during fingerprinting. Each rule combines glob patterns with min/max byte thresholds.

patterns.description: List of glob patterns to apply the min/max size rule

min.description: Minimum size of the file in bytes

max.description: Maximum size of the file in bytes

proxy.description: Proxy configuration for file snippet requests

http_config.description: HTTP configuration for file snippet requests

base_uri.description: Base URI for file snippet API requests

ignore_cert_errors.description: Whether to ignore TLS/SSL certificate errors. Intended for testing and development environments only; do not enable in production.

ranking_threshold.description: Ranking threshold for file snippet results (range 0–10). A value of `-1` defers to server configuration. Higher values require stronger matches.

honour_file_exts.description: When `true`, the server considers file extensions during matching (e.g. a `.c` file only matches other `.c` files). When `false`, file extensions are ignored. When `null`, defers to server configuration.

skip_headers.description: Skip license headers, comments, and imports at the beginning of files before snippet matching. Works together with `skip_headers_limit` to control how many leading lines are stripped.

skip_headers_limit.description: Maximum number of leading lines to strip when `skip_headers` is enabled. A value of `0` means no limit (strip all detected header lines).

hpfm.description: HPFM (High Precision Folder Matching) configuration. HPFM detects copied directory structures by comparing folder-level fingerprints.

ranking_threshold.description: Ranking threshold for HPFM results (range 0–99). Note: this range differs from `file_snippet.ranking_threshold` (0–10). A value of `-1` defers to server configuration. Higher values require stronger matches.

bom.description: BOM Rules: Set of rules that will be used to modify the BOM before and after the scan is completed

include.description: Rules for adding components as scan context. Sent to the SCANOSS API to influence result matching. Requires purl; path is optional for partial matching.

path.description: File or folder path, relative to the scan root. Paths ending with `/` are treated as folder rules and match all files under that directory.

purl.description: Package URL identifying the component. Format: `pkg:<type>/<namespace>/<name>@<version>`. Version is recommended but optional.

acknowledgement.description: Formal acknowledgement of this BOM decision, propagated to SBOM output

remove.description: Rules for removing components from results after scanning (client-side post-processing). Supports full match (both path and purl) or partial match (path only or purl only).

path.description: File or folder path, relative to the scan root. Paths ending with `/` are treated as folder rules and match all files under that directory.

purl.description: Package URL identifying the component to remove. Format: `pkg:<type>/<namespace>/<name>@<version>`. Version is recommended but optional.

acknowledgement.description: Formal acknowledgement of this BOM decision, propagated to SBOM output

examples.comment: Full match: removes only this purl at this path
examples.acknowledgement: Reviewed and confirmed as original code, not third-party

examples.comment: Partial match: removes this purl wherever it appears
Partial match: removes all results under this folder

replace.description: Rules for replacing components in results after scanning (client-side post-processing). Requires both purl (original component) and replace_with (replacement). Path is optional for scoping.

path.description: File or folder path, relative to the scan root. Paths ending with `/` are treated as folder rules and match all files under that directory.

purl.description: Package URL of the original component to replace. Format: `pkg:<type>/<namespace>/<name>@<version>`. Version is recommended but optional.

acknowledgement.description: Formal acknowledgement of this BOM decision, propagated to SBOM output

license.description: License of the replacement component. Should be a valid SPDX license expression (e.g. `MIT`, `Apache-2.0`, `GPL-3.0-only`).

examples.acknowledgement: Verified upstream project is the correct attribution
